spec_match.rationale: No-developer-visible-change feature — by definition the API doesn't expose the new behaviour, so the demo can only probe indirectly. Honest about the limit but doesn't fully match the spec's framing.

interactive.rationale: Fit/overflow mode buttons and a reset control now let visitors switch between the non-overflowing containment case and a normal overflowing scroller while watching the body scroll counter.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Either the feature-index blurb or the concept lede is missing — can't verify alignment.

edge_cases.rationale: Demo runs the happy path; no visible feature detection, error handling, or no-support fallback in the script.

references.rationale: Page includes the chromestatus.com/feature/5129635997941760 link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: The touched filler styling now uses shared CSS variables, and the controls inherit the global button color pair.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: showing a non-overflowing container blocking propagation
openQuestions.detail: The respect-overscroll-behavior-on-non-scrollable-scroll-containers feature's headline framing — overscroll-behavior applies to scroll containers even when they don't currently overflow — backdrop / popover use cases — calls out "showing a non-overflowing container blocking propagation". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: comparison with the previous behaviour
openQuestions.detail: The respect-overscroll-behavior-on-non-scrollable-scroll-containers feature's headline framing — overscroll-behavior applies to scroll containers even when they don't currently overflow — backdrop / popover use cases — calls out "comparison with the previous behaviour". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

openQuestions.title: Align the concept lede with the feature-index blurb
openQuestions.detail: The blurb on the feature index sells one promise; the concept lede frames a different angle. Either reword the lede to echo the blurb's keywords (so visitors recognise they're in the right place) or update the parent blurb.

summary: non-scrollable-overscroll under respect-overscroll-behavior-on-non-scrollable-scroll-containers: 3 pass / 3 partial / 0 fail. The page now has explicit fit/overflow controls and no touched raw-color drift; comparison and fallback follow-ups remain.
